observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to:

i. Retrieve all necessary verified that there
documents of the dorman there
and cause the collection of the amounts due from continuous issuance of demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and clarifications on the grant of loans to the borrowers/beneficiaries, collection of unpaid loan balances from the co-makers who guaranteed the repayment of loan and sending regularly of statement of accounts to borrowers;

ii. Strengthen record-keeping practices to ensure that all receivable accounts are adequately supported with documents and records with complete details;

b. Avail of the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts by following the guidelines and procedures on the proper disposition thereof to present fairly the receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management has verified that there are no available records on file for the receivables from the MAO and COMELEC. Furthermore, a Certification was issued by the Provincial Accountant in November 2023 confirming that there is no existing balance of "Due to LGU–Agno" in the books of the province.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Review the transactions recorded in the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to determine the details of the accounting errors which caused the unreconciled difference of Due to Other ₱251,969.05; and

b. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry using the appropriate accounts to present the accurate balances of the reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 3049 in December 2020 to zero out the balances of the reciprocal accounts. However, due to the unavailability of supporting documents—both from municipal records and COA—no further action was taken to substantiate or reconcile the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Review and rectify the inaccuracies in the presentation of changes in the Final Budget, particularly regarding the additions and changes in current appropriations;

b. Analyze the transactions affecting the balance of Continuing Appropriations and make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancy between the ending balance in CY 2022 and forwarded balances in CY 2023 in the amount of ₱10,208,353.62; and

c. Reconcile the actual amounts of MOOE in the SCBAA with the MOOE in the SFPer, and the amount reflected in the actual amount of SCBAA under CO and in the SFPer, and disclose the details of reconciling items in the NTFS to provide reliable information to the users of the financial statements and also enable the Municipality to identify the major source of difference and improve its performance in the discharge of its accountability obligations.
observations.managementAction: No action has been taken by Management. The deficiencies remain unaddressed, and the inaccuracies in the budget presentation continue to affect the reliability of the estimated revenue, receipts, and appropriations in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership over the lots through TCT in the name of the Municipal Government; 

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to reconcile their records pertaining to the parcels of land owned by the Municipality and record the two identified real properties in the name of the Municipality; and

c. Require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to assess and address constraints related to the registration of the land and prioritize budget allocation for land registration within the fiscal constraints to expedite the process.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor confirmed that all untitled lots have already been submitted to the subdivided. As a result, the titling process has become more complex due to the requirement of an approved subdivision plan. An amount of ₱150,000 was initially allocated by the Municipal Budget Officer for land titling under the CY 2024 Budget; however, it was subsequently reallocated to another purpose and no disbursement or obligation was made for land titling as of the validation date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately stop the practice of paying the services, supplies and materials through reimbursement basis and to require payments for government transactions to be made by check and payable directly to the creditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are taking steps to stop the practice. However, when reimbursement is unavoidable, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer deduct the corresponding tax from the payee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management formulate and issue policies or guidelines for the implementation of the AICS program of the Municipality which will provide clearly who are entitled, the amount to be given depending on the current situation or condition of the recipient, the number of times the financial assistance could be availed by the same recipient in a year and the needed documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO confirmed that a draft of policies/guidelines has been formulated and submitted for review by the Municipal Mayor. No documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

The HRMA already deducted unadjusted balances from other concerned employees. The HRMA conducted monthly postings on leave cards. The HRMA has conducted monthly postings on leave cards; however, some leave cards were incomplete and improperly accomplished. The HRMA was promoted as Senior Public Employment Service Officer during the second quarter of CY 2025. To date, the position remains vacant. The Municipal Accountant sent several letters to the HRMA to rectify the leave balances. A follow-up letter will be prepared to be signed by the new LCE for compliance of the findings as enumerated. Management commits to fill-up the vacant HRMA position soonest to be able to submit the updated leave cards including the unsubmitted leave cards of 14 personnel that required by the previous Audit Team Leader.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the BAC Chairman, in coordination with the BAC Secretariat, to furnish the Audit Team copies of the POs together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period to enable timely review and communication to Management of deficiencies that may be noted in the evaluation thereof of the Audit Team.

b. Ensure that every PO is duly filled-out with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers and Management in complying with the terms and conditions thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted POs; however, only a few were submitted within the prescribed deadlines. Some POs lacked essential details such as dates, delivery terms, and payment terms.

The Municipal Treasurer and her team visited 17 barangays of Agno to strengthen tax collection through information drives. The Municipal Treasurer introduced incentives to encourage early payment of taxes. Despite the incentives, a significant amount of taxes remained uncollected.

In their response letter dated May 26, 2025, the Management mentioned that Water Resource Development is at No. 2 under the Five Point Development Agenda of the newly elected LCE, hence signified their intention to comply. To date, there is only one regular staff under AWWS, an Admin. Aide III who used to be a JO for a long time plus a designated clerk (Admin Aide IV) whose plantilla position is under the Mayor’s Office. The LGU will revisit the rates and implement the corresponding adjustment once the water supply is stable. The Municipality will study the possibility of shifting from manual to automated billing and revisit its policy concerning disconnections. The salary of AWWS staff has been in the Mayor’s Office (MO) Budget with effect from January 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the MPDC to plan well the PPAs under the 20 percent DF that are procurement-and-implementation-ready and essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the constituents for an efficient execution thereof. We also recommended that Management require the MPDC to closely monitor the timeline of implementation of the different development PPAs identified in the approved development plan.
observations.managementAction: Projects were not implemented in CY 2024. Management committed to pursue the projects in the ensuing year. Eight PPAs for CY 2024 totaling ₱7,160,152.14 remained unimplemented as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review the under/over remittance of premiums and take appropriate actions. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, ensure the timely and accurate remittance of premium contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances which may result in incurrence of interest and penalties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant encountered difficulties in reconciling records due to the absence of complete documentation of prior years. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer remitted premium contributions. However, the Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts reflect unremitted balances of ₱1,710,229.83, ₱225,498.88 and ₱76,746.49 respectively, as of January 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to restate the affected accounts for all ensuing financial statements, if there is any, to fairly present the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to restate the accounts, however, access to data dating as far back as 2019 is required for thorough review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the conduct of physical count of all its PPE through the Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members and reconcile the inventory count with property and accounting records through the Property/Treasury and Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was reorganized through EO No. 049 dated June 6, 2022. Additionally, Ms. Jaclin V. Navalta has been appointed as Property Custodian effective July 16, 2022. Memorandum No. 016-2022 dated August 16, 2022 was issued to all Department Heads for the conduct of inventory of office equipment and fixtures in every office in preparation for the physical count of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the estimated Revenue and Receipts in the amount of ₱194,846,760.00 in the Original Budget for Current Appropriations in the SCBAA consistent with the approved Annual Budget per Appropriation Ordinance No. 2021-06, Series of 2021 and Municipal School Board Ordinance No. 2021-03, Series of 2021 to present fairly the balance of appropriations originally authorized and the additions to and other changes in the budgets through the six Supplemental Budgets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to adjust the accounts, however, access to data before 2019 is required for thorough review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to submit application for transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority to establish absolute ownership and make a follow-up on the schedule of ocular inspection with the CENRO.
observations.managementAction: Five parcels of land had been transferred in the name of the Municipality. Of the 49 lots, only five parcels of land have been transferred in the name of the Municipality.

Management plans to procure the necessary equipment along with the construction of the wastewater treatment plant and additional disposal facilities for waste disposal upon approval of the loan. However, the NTP from the DENR has not yet been issued. The Municipality has yet to procure the necessary equipment and funds. The Municipality hired an Environmental Specialist I to oversee the operation of the Sanitary Landfill.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management require the HRMA to prepare and transmit promptly the required forms to the GSIS to update the personal records of employees both in the Municipality and GSIS to ensure full remittance of premiums to the GSIS and secure the social security benefits of municipal employees.
observations.managementAction: Management visited GSIS Dagupan City to inquire about the issue. Apparently, the amount withheld was not remitted in full on the month. The difference was remitted in the following month. Based on records, the amount issued was remitted in full on the following month.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management insure the waterworks facilities, tourist sheds and NCDC and other properties relevant in the delivery of public service to provide safeguard against substantial losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, properties brought by fortuitous events by requiring the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to identify the insurable assets and to submit the inventory report to the GSIS and Audit Team. Likewise, instruct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds needed for the payment of premiums.
observations.managementAction: Management only insured motor vehicles and five buildings of the Municipality. Management did not insure all properties relevant in the delivery of public service leaving properties vulnerable to losses in the event of unforeseen events.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person, with the assistance of the MPDC, to spearhead and prioritize the preparation of adequate and systematic GAD Database which will aid in the identification of gender issues and full implementation of the PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget to attain the main intentions in promoting gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfillment of women’s human rights and the promotion of women’s economic empowerment.
observations.managementAction: The updating of the GAD database is still on-going. The Audit Team was informed that they are still in the process of gathering the necessary information on gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data needed for the database in coordination with the MPDC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer, to impose the additional LD in the amount of ₱68,428.67 on the delays incurred in the Construction of Potable Water System Level II in Barangay Patar and impose the liquidated damages amounting to ₱3,888.01 on the delays incurred in the Construction of Potable Water System Level II in Barangay Macaboboni and to require the contractors to submit the written consent of bondsmen for the extension of contract time; and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Engineer, to submit the lacking documents to support claims and for review of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant imposed the liquidated damages amounting to ₱68,428.67 to CLP Builders of Bani, Pangasinan. A copy of the letter with proof of receipt was submitted to the Audit Team last May 27, 2022. A reminder letter will be sent to the above-mentioned contractor. The Municipal has yet to submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the BAC to submit the lacking documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Treasurer, to submit the lacking documents to support claims and for review of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages to contractors of the five projects and collect the same by deducting from any money due or which may become due to them and to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the Request for Time Extension and Statement of Work Accomplished as of the target completion date for the project of Construction of PCCP at Purok Arinaar to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted all possible means/efforts to collect the liquidated damages of the five projects. The liquidated damages of the five projects remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, to submit the required authorization from the SB and other supporting documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to submit the required authorization from the SB and other supporting documents. The required documents were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO, to issue demand letters to the 17 recipients of financial subsidy who were not listed in the Certificate of Eligibility and who were employed in the government and private sector to cause the refund of financial assistance totaling ₱93,500.00 and to return the amounts collected to the DSWD Regional Office.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to the beneficiaries. Management was unable to recover the financial subsidy from the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC and BAC Secretariat to submit immediately the contracts and supporting documents of 13 projects with total contract cost of ₱16,200,011.91 to the Audit Team and to instruct the responsible officers to observe the prescribed period of submission of five working days from the execution of a contract to gain the benefits of facilitating the review and evaluation process and obtaining suggested corrective measures on the defects and deficiencies noted in the contract and its supporting documents which are relevant in decision making. We recommended that Management take necessary appropriate administrative disciplinary action as against the responsible employee for unjustified non-compliance with the Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management sent letters to the concerned parties to explain the inordinate delay in the submission of contracts. Verification of the submitted bid documents revealed that the submission was beyond the prescribed period and also some bidding documents were still not submitted to the Audit Team. Management has not taken any administrative disciplinary action as against the responsible employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the official designated in the issuance of driver’s trip ticket to ensure that the necessary data are duly provided and prepare the Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to improve existing policies on the use of government vehicles and control fuel withdrawals and consumption by writing a letter to the personnel in charge in issuing gas slips. The required documents were not submitted in the post audit of disbursement vouchers for the payment of gasoline expenses of the Municipality, the Audit Team noted that the required reports were not prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents to support the propriety of the transactions, subject to further evaluation of the Audit Team to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to submit the lacking documents. The BAC is currently collating the lacking documents and committed to submit the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the supplier through the Municipal Treasurer to perform the necessary customizations to enable the use of the cashiering system in the collection of receipts and generation of reports in the prescribed format consistent with the terms and conditions of the contract. We also reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the cost of the undelivered goods, if any, and unperformed customizations as of March 17, 2020 and impose liquidated damages against the supplier for the delays incurred in accordance with the provision of Section 3 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 02-2020 dated February 27, 2020.
observations.managementAction: No action has been taken by Management. The supplier was unable to perform the necessary customizations needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and to instruct the BAC and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445. We further recommended that the Management impose liquidated damages against the supplier concerned by undertaking all the remedies to mitigate the effects of delay in the project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Management required the BAC to submit written comments. The required documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the required documents be submitted by the officials and employees concerned to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management had been very strict in completing the documents for disbursements under SEF. However, some outputs of the consultants were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC/BAC Secretariat/Municipal Engineer/Municipal Accountant to submit (1) immediately the required documents; (2) justification/explanation on the noted lapses; and (3) adhere strictly to the above-cited laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted some of the lacking documents embodied in the audit observation. No explanation was submitted by Management regarding the noted lapses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management submit the complete documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of contract and for the payment of claims.
observations.managementAction: Management already instructed the BAC Secretariat to submit all the supporting documents. The BAC has partially submitted the lacking documents as of this date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC submit the complete documentary requirements for the proper review and evaluation of the negotiated procurement (small value procurement) and for the payment of claims.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat committed to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and IRR of RA No. 9184. Partial submission of the lacking documents had been made by Management.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management submit immediately the required documents to adhere with COA laws, rules and regulations in order to protect the best interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management had been very strict in completing the documents for disbursements under SEF. However, some documents were incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
to record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct
balance of the accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the:
2.10.1 Municipal Accountant to:
2.10.1.1 Prepare an Inventory Listing of the PPE assets to be
transferred, based on accounting records and furnish
a copy to the Municipal Engineer and Municipal
Property Custodian;
2.10.1.2 Prepare JEV to record the derecognition of the PPE
and corresponding recognition of donations duly
supported with necessary documents, such as but not
limited to SB Resolution, Deed of Donation and
Certificate of Acceptance by the recipient agency;
2.10.1.3 Provide the accountant of the respective Schools
Division Office/DepEd copies of the JEVs and its
supporting documents for proper recognition and
recording in their books of accounts;
2.10.2 Municipal Engineer and Municipal Property Custodian to
conduct a physical inventory of the assets to verify their
existence and condition, prepare an Inventory Report of the
assets to be transferred and provide the said report to the
Municipal Accountant together with the Certificate of
Completion and Acceptance; and
2.10.3 Municipal Property Custodian to secure authorization from
the SB authorizing the transfer of the identified school
buildings and other completed structures to the respective
Schools Division Office/DepEd, being the rightful recipient
agencies with control and use of said properties based on the
listing of properties provided by the Municipal Accountant
duly validated by the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to:
3.3.1 Verify the PPE transactions to ensure proper classification and
accurate reporting of the amount under investing activities;
3.3.2 Provide full disclosure and explanation of the “Other
Adjustments” in the reconciliation of net cash flows presented
in the NTFS; and
3.3.3 Update the NTFS to reflect the change in accounting policy
and submit a comprehensive schedule that includes all
derecognized PPEs and their reclassification as semi-
expendable expenses under all applicable funds.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to:
4.7.1 Review and adjust the inaccuracies in the presentation of
changes in the Final Budget, particularly regarding the
additions and changes in current appropriations;
4.7.2 Analyze the transactions affecting the balance of Continuing
Appropriations and make the necessary adjustments on the
discrepancy between the ending balance in CY 2023 and
forwarded balance in CY 2024 in the amount of ₱7,726,131.21;
and
4.7.3 Reclassify the Grants and Donations in Kind amounting to
₱52,768.93 and properly report it as a reconciling item
between the SCBAA and SFPer under Non-Cash Items/Income
not considered budgetary items and conduct a thorough
reconciliation of revenue accounts between the SCBAA and
SFPer to ensure consistency and reliability of reported
financial information.

We recommended that Management:
5.4.1 Instruct the Municipal Property Custodian to:
5.4.1.1 Prepare and maintain property cards;
5.4.1.2 Prepare an updated list of accountable officers and
update the PAR every three years or every time there
is a change in custodianship;
5.4.2 Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory
of all properties owned by the Municipality and render report
thereon within the prescribed period; and
5.4.3 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Property
Custodian to coordinate and initiate compliance with the
implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances through the creation of an Inventory Committee by
the Head of the Agency.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Present the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the amount of ₱1,659,099.96 and ₱1,520,841.99 under the Current Liability and Non-Current Liability respectively.

b. Reflect the accurate balance of the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Long Term Borrowings balance is now ₱1,520,841.99 from January 2024. Management has ensured that the accurate balance of the Loans Payable – Domestic account is now properly reflected in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to ensure compliance of bidders with the eligibility requirements stipulated in the relevant procurement rules and regulations, particularly regarding the validity of the PCAB license, including the ARCC, before declaring the bidder compliant to ensure that government contracts are awarded only to qualified bidders.
observations.managementAction: Management has strengthened BAC procedures to ensure strict compliance with bidders’ eligibility criteria. The BAC now verifies compliance of the bidders with PCAB license validity and ARCC requirements to ensure qualified government contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to include the list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption of the current year to provide sufficient bases in the use of funds for the implementation of the projects and activities supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council within the next five years. We also recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to take immediate action to implement the aforementioned projects to enhance its disaster preparedness and response capabilities and to monitor and evaluate these projects to ensure the effectiveness of measures in barangay safeguarding the community against potential disasters.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO has identified the specific projects to be funded. The letter dated April 29, 2024, did not specify which projects would be covered. Additionally, the Audit Team requested a detailed explanation for the adjustments made during implementation to assess the appropriateness of the unexpended LDRRMF balances of previous years. The MDRRMO implemented the projects, which were carried out and monitored to enhance disaster preparedness. The municipal siren was repaired at no cost. Additionally, CBDRRM training was conducted in collaboration with Agno PNP and BFP, while CBDRRM training was integrated into general assemblies.

These will serve as a guide to efficient and effective management of the waterworks system and to achieve the goal of providing potable water to households and maximize the use of the resources to generate additional income to the LGU. 

We also recommended that Management instruct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to provide the necessary budget for the upgrading of the Potable Water Systems in the Barangays of Allabon, Cayungnan, Patar and Macaboboni to Level III in order to increase the number of households benefited by the projects.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer committed to adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160 to ensure collection of delinquent accounts on realty taxes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to request from all depository banks complete copies of Credit Memo and Debit Memo every month; and to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the reconciling items duly supported with documents in the books of accounts.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant process the replacement of stale checks consistent with the provisions of Section 59, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card and to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year the list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the current year. Based on the list, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and the corresponding deferred income accounts to present the accurate balance of the said receivable accounts in the financial statements.
We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to update and reconcile the balance of the delinquent real property tax with the accounts of the taxpayers in the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions recorded in the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts to determine the accounting errors which caused the unreconciled difference of ₱147,098.95 and to prepare the necessary accounting journal entries using the appropriate accounts prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs to present the accurate balances of reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in retrieving all necessary documents of the dormant accounts and cause the collection of the amounts due through issuance of demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and clarifications on the grant of loan to the borrowers/beneficiaries, collection of unpaid loan balances from the co-makers who guaranteed the re-payment of loan and sending regularly of statement of accounts to borrowers.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer, to reconcile their records with the records of the Provincial Accounting Office on Due to LGUs account to facilitate collection of cash deposits amounting to ₱226,079.32 from the Provincial Treasury.
We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the conditions and requirements provided in Sections 7.4 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to facilitate the write-off of the dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer or the designated Supply Officer, if any, to prepare and submit the required SSMI with the original copies of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the recording/adjustments of expenditures on supplies and materials in the books of accounts. We further recommended that the accounting procedures provided in Paragraph 44 of IPSAS 12 and Sections 114 and 121, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs be strictly observed in the purchase and issuance of inventory items in the ensuing year.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the ICO-Municipal Assessor, to cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership over the parcels of land owned by the LGU and to require the ICO-Municipal Assessor to submit application for transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority to establish absolute ownership to protect the interest of the LGU.
We further recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which shall cover the expenses for the registration of the parcels of land.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to recognize the receipt of the machineries costing ₱940,800.00 from the Department of Agriculture and to provide depreciation costs in the amount of ₱37,240.00 to present fairly the affected asset, income and expense accounts in the financial statements.
The following is the suggested journal entry:
Machinery 					₱940,800.00
Depreciation – Machinery and Equipment		     37,240.00
Grants and Donations in Kind				₱940,800.00
Accumulated Depreciation – Machinery		                           37,240.00

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger cards for each group of PPE and the subsidiary records for roads and road component for every road network and for the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the equivalent property cards for PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of the same and the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components which are necessary to support the accuracy and reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended that Management require the conduct of physical count of all its PPE through the Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members and to reconcile the inventory count with property and accounting records through the Property/Treasury and Accounting Unit consistent with the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the liabilities incurred for overtime pay, gratuity pay, travel expenses and reimbursements of other expenses of officials and employees under the Due to Officers and Employees account and the unpaid financial assistance to beneficiaries of AICS Program under the Other Payables account to present fairly the balances of the said accounts and the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the description of accounts provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure use of appropriate accounts in recording financial transactions.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to recognize the error in prior year due to non-recognition of revenue for the receipt of the motor vehicle donated by the Department of Health-Center for Health Development I in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity through the Prior Period Adjustments account.
The following is the suggested journal entry:
Government Equity				₱2,350,000.00
Prior Period Adjustments			₱2,350,000.00

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Current Appropriation for PS, MOOE and CO in the total net amount of ₱19,004,121.52 presented as Original Budget in the SCBAA consistent with the Current Appropriation in the approved Annual Budget per Appropriation Ordinance No. 2020-06 series of 2020 to present fairly the balance of appropriations originally authorized and the additions to and other changes in the budgets through the eight Supplemental Budget in the financial statements;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the transactions affecting the balance of Continuing Appropriation and to make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancy between the ending balance in CY 2020 and forwarded balance in CY 2021 in the amount of ₱3,217,800.86; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the actual amount for Personal Services in the amount of ₱824,000.00 to present the appropriations used for the SRI in the SCBAA.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages to contractors of the five projects and collect the same by deducting from any money due or which may become due to them and to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the Request for Time Extension and Statement of Work Accomplished as of the target completion date for the project of Construction of PCCP at Purok Arinaar to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, to submit the required authorization from the SB and other supporting documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
We further recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that obligations for generic PPAs are supported with SB Resolution authorizing the Local Chief Executive to enter in contract and specifying the projects thereon before certifying the Obligation Requests as to existence of available appropriations.

We recommended that Management require the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to determine the conformity of the programs, projects and activities to be charged against the 20% DF with the guidelines provided in DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No.1 dated November 4, 2020 before certifying the availability of allotment to ensure strict compliance in the utilization of the 20 percent DF.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper charges to the fund are made.

We recommended that Management require the OIC Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the expenditures to be charged against the LDRRMF are in conformity with the guidelines before certifying the availability of appropriations and allotment to ensure that the calamity fund is utilized solely for expenditures related to disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended that Management require the OIC Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Finance Committee to:
Refrain from using the appropriations for PS, MOOE and CO to augment the appropriation of any expense item belonging to another expense class;
Avoid the reversion of appropriations unless it pertains to reversion of unexpended balances of appropriations to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year through supplemental budget and the reversion of continuing appropriations unless obligations therefor have been fully paid or otherwise settled; and
Prepare the Supplemental Budget in accordance with the requirements of Section 321 of RA No. 7160.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO, to issue demand letters to the 17 recipients of financial subsidy who were not listed in the Certificate of Eligibility and who were employed in the government and private sector to cause the refund of financial assistance totaling ₱93,500.00 and to return the amounts collected to the DSWD Regional Office.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and BAC Secretariat to submit immediately the contracts and supporting documents of 13 projects with total contract cost of ₱16,200,011.91 to the Audit Team and to instruct the responsible officers to observe the prescribed period of submission of five working days from the execution of a contract to gain the benefits of facilitating the review and evaluation process and obtaining suggested corrective measures on the defects and deficiencies noted in the contract and its supporting documents which are relevant in decision making.
We further recommended that Management take necessary appropriate administrative disciplinary action as against the responsible employee for unjustified non-compliance with the Circular.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management instruct the Chairman of the BAC to require from suppliers the submission of the mandatory documents in the use of alternative method of Small Value Procurement to ensure that the most advantageous price to the LGU are obtained from suppliers of known qualifications, and to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents and ensure that all of the documentary requirements are complied with.

We recommended that Management:
Require the official designated in the issuance of driver’s trip ticket to ensure that the necessary data are duly provided and prepare the Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and
Direct the drivers to properly accomplish the portion of the driver’s trip ticket to be filled-up by the driver indicating all the necessary information and ensure that passengers will sign the certification portion that the vehicle was used for official travel.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Human Resource and Management Assistant (HRMA) to ensure that DTRs are duly signed by the payees and the immediate supervisor and accomplishment reports of JOWs are submitted every payroll cut-off;
Require the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant, to furnish the Audit Team with the copy of payroll duly signed by all payees; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify the completeness of supporting documents before affixing her signature in the disbursement vouchers for the payment of wages, allowances and other similar expenses.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 to support the propriety of the transactions, subject to further evaluation of the Audit Team to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management require the supplier through the Municipal Treasurer to perform the necessary customizations to enable the use of the cashiering system in the collection of receipts and generation of reports in the prescribed format consistent with the terms and conditions of the contract.
We also reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the cost of the undelivered goods, if any, and unperformed customizations as of March 17, 2020 and impose liquidated damages against the supplier for the delays incurred in accordance with the provision of Section 3 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 02-2020 dated February 27, 2020.

We recommended that Management create an organizational structure which will outline the flow of activities and define the roles and responsibilities of the personnel assigned in the waterworks system consistent with Resolution No. 079-2001 and Ordinance No. 2001-02 dated March 5, 2001. We also recommended that Management require the designated Chief of Section with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant, to formulate written policies and procedures in the operation of the water system which shall serve as a guide to efficient and effective management of the waterworks system and to achieve the goal of providing potable water to households and maximize the use of resources in generating additional income for the LGU.
We further recommended that Management review the rates per cubic meter of water consumption provided in Resolution No. 079-2001 and Ordinance No. 2001-002 dated March 5, 2001 to determine its reasonableness as compared with the existing rates of nearby water districts or water facilities and with the personal services and maintenance and operating expenses incurred in the operation of the water system which shall serve as a basis in implementing an increase and improvement of delivery services.

We recommended that Management:
Require the ICO-MPDC, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer, to plan well and ensure that all PPAs under the 20% DF are procurement-and-implementation ready for an efficient execution thereof;
Instruct the Municipal Engineer, with the assistance of the ICO-MPDC, to come up with a time frame in the implementation of the development projects to deliver the desired benefits to the constituents on time and attain desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of LGU; and
Require the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to adhere with the existing laws, rules and regulations in the realignment of funds.

We recommended that Management ensure the collection of delinquent real property taxes by requiring the Municipal Treasurer to apply the remedies provided in Sections 254, 256, 257, 258 and 260 of RA No. 7160.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to make information drive campaigns such as posting the notice of the dates when the tax may be paid with discounts and without interest at conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the Municipal Hall to encourage taxpayers to pay real property taxes in advance or on time in the ensuing years to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the LGU geared towards the welfare of the constituents.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person, with the assistance of the ICO MPDC, to spearhead the preparation of adequate and systematic GAD Database which will aid in the identification of gender issues and full implementation of the PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget to attain the main intentions in promoting gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfilment of women’s human rights and the promotion of women’s economic empowerment.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management require the HRMA to prepare and transmit promptly the required forms to the GSIS to update the personal records of employees both in the LGU and GSIS to ensure full remittance of premiums to the GSIS.
We further recommended that Management, through the HRMA in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, inform in writing the Head of the GSIS of the discrepancies on the personnel records between the LGU and GSIS database to come up with corrective measures which will enable the remittance of premiums in full to the GSIS and also secure the social security benefits of municipal employees.

We recommended that Management instruct the HRMA to revise the standard/pro-forma contracts of JOWs and indicate the contract duration and that services rendered are not credited as government service. We also recommended that attestation provided in Item b, Section 4 of CSC Resolution No. 021480 and acknowledgment of the contract by the JOWs be required for proper conveyance of terms and stipulations indicated therein.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that Management insure the waterworks facilities, Tourist Sheds and NCDC and other properties relevant in the delivery of public service to provide safeguard against substantial losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, properties brought by fortuitous events by requiring the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to identify the insurable assets and to submit the inventory report to the GSIS and Audit Team consistent with the provision of  COA Circular No. 2018 - 002 dated May 31, 2018; and to instruct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds needed for the payment of premiums.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry to recognize the amount of uncollected real property tax in CY 2017, 2018 and 2019 in the books of accounts to present fairly the balances of the RPT and SET receivables accounts and Deferred RPT and Deferred SET accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.  Ensure the adherence to the provision of Section 16 of RA 9003, otherwise known as “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” by causing the immediate completion and approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan which shall serve as the foundation, guide and timetable in the identification, implementation and operation of solid waste management programs; and


b.  Require the concerned officials and employees to strictly observe the criteria provided in Sections 41 and 42 of RA 9003 in the establishment and operation of Sanitary Landfill to ensure the protection of public health and its environment.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with National Waste Management Commission (NWMC) and DENR Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) for the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan
observations.reason:
Interview with the focal persons for solid waste management disclosed that the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU was not yet approved by the NWMC due to absence of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS). Reiterated in Observation No.23, Page 93-96 of the report.



The Sanitary Landfill is not yet in operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert utmost effort to submit the accounts and financial reports within the prescribed period for timely examination and verification of accounts and financial report in compliance with Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts, Section 39 (1) and (3) of PD No. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office and Treasury Office exerted best efforts to submit the reports and accounts within the prescribed period despite of the effects of COVID-19 situation.
observations.reason: Records showed that the accounts and reports were not submitted on the 10th day of the ensuing month. However, in consideration of the effects of COVID-19 and implementation of work schedules, the audit observation was not reiterated in the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure compliance with the requirements prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and cause the submission of copies of contracts to the Audit Team to enable the auditor to conduct timely review and technical evaluation of the contracts. 

Further, take appropriate administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 4 of the same Circular against the responsible officer concerned for unjustified non-compliance with the Circular.

observations.reason: Delays of 148 to 279 days were incurred in the submission of copies of contracts and supporting documents of nine projects with total contract cost of ₱35,234,160.28 while the contracts and bid documents of the other nine projects with total contract cost of ₱11,825,502.98 remained unsubmitted as of December 31, 2020. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure collection of the delinquent accounts on realty taxes by adopting the procedures and remedies provided in Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991” and employing all legal measures to enable the collection of unpaid stall rentals, market electricity and water bills to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the Municipality geared towards the welfare of the constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure timely remittance of premiums in adherence to Section 6(b) of RA No. 8291; otherwise, known as GSIS Act of 1997, by requiring the Human Resource and Management Office to transmit promptly to the GSIS the required forms in updating the records of the personnel; and in coordination with other offices concerned, to update the personnel records of employees both in the LGU and GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the reports to updated records in the GSIS.
observations.reason: Verification of records showed that the monthly personal share of employees and the contribution for employer’s share were not remitted in full amount due to the discrepancies between the records of the personnel of the LGU and the GSIS database. observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that management see to it that Gender and Development (GAD) Plan of the agency will fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives towards the role of women in becoming a participative partner in nation building and development, with the end view of protecting their lives and rights and to live a modest life in a society.

observations.recommendation:
1.2 Municipality of Agno failed to deduct 10% and/or 1% retention money from the progress payment made to the contractor/supplier contrary to Section 6, Annex E and Section 62.1 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended           that management take the following courses of action:

Submit the complete documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of contract and for the payment of claims in compliance with COA Memo Circular No. 2005-027 and COA Circular No. 2012-001. 

Ensure that 10% retention money is being deducted from the progress payment to the contractor to serve as warranty for hidden defects during the construction period and shall only be released upon final acceptance of the project and/or if the contractor request for the substitution of the retention money for each progress billing; and 1%retention money deducted from the total contract price for the payment of delivered goods and shall only be released after the lapse of the warranty period.

Require the BAC Secretariat to attach a checklist on all perfected contracts as guide to those employees collating the documents before submission to the Office of the Auditor.  Likewise, for the Municipal Accountant to also attach checklist on disbursement vouchers and see to it that complete supporting documents were present before payments are made.
observations.managementAction:
Management had already instructed the BAC Secretariat to submit all the lacking supporting documents. The BAC has partially submitted the lacking documents as of this date.





Management had already deducting the 10% and/or 1% retention money from progress payments.
observations.reason:
Per verification of the Audit Team, Management had partially submitted the lacking documents. 





Per verification of the audit team, Management had started deducting the retention money from the progress payments made to the contractors.





The submitted contracts and supporting documents are enumerated in the Transmittal Letter.

We recommended that Management:

Require the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to determine the conformity of the PPAs to be charged against the LDRRMF and MDF with the provisions of the DBM Circulars before certifying the availability of allotment;
Require the BAC, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to post all NOA, contract, or purchase order in the website of the LGU, if any, or any conspicuous place in the premises of the Procuring Entity and GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act for transparency purposes and to require the submission of the mandatory documentary requirements from the suppliers, distributors, manufacturers or contractors concerned; and
Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO and MHO, to submit the list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution of relief goods, medicines and vitamins and other supporting documents.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer, to submit the copy of valid ID, Barangay Certification with specimen signature of the beneficiary and authorization letter of the beneficiaries provided in the schedule for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO, to issue demand letters to the 14 recipients of financial subsidy who were not listed in the Certificate of Eligibility and to the three beneficiaries who were employed to government and private sector to cause the refund of financial assistance totaling ₱93,500.00 and return the amounts collected to the DSWD.

We recommended that Management require the TWG and BAC to verify the accuracy of the NFCC and other eligibility requirements submitted by the bidders in order to ensure the award of government contracts to qualified bidders.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages in the amount of ₱143,090.00 to the Supplier for the delays incurred in accordance with the provision of Section 3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Statement of Work Accomplished as of the target completion date to the Audit Team and instruct the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages to Contractors in accordance with the provision of Section 68, Rule XXII of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Chairman of the BAC, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to submit immediately the contracts and supporting documents of nine projects with total contract cost of ₱11,825,502.98 to the Audit Team and to observe the prescribed period of submission of five (5) working days from the execution of a contract in order to gain the benefits of facilitating the review and evaluation process and obtaining suggested corrective measures on the defects and deficiencies noted in the contract and its supporting documents which are relevant in decision making.

We recommended that Management instruct the Chairman of the BAC to require from suppliers the submission of the mandatory documents in the use of alternative method of Small Value Procurement to ensure that the most advantageous price to the LGU are obtained from suppliers of known qualifications and to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents and see to it that all of the documentary requirements are duly dated and complete to ensure transparency and propriety of the procurement transactions.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor to cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the parcels of land owned by the LGU. After which, require the Municipal Assessor to submit application for transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority to establish absolute ownership in accordance with Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and Section 449, Volume I of GAAM and protect the interest of LGU. We further recommended that Management require the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which shall cover the expenses on the registration of parcels of land.

We recommended that Management require the designated employee or official in the issuance of driver’s trip ticket to see to it that the required information are duly indicated before issuance and to oblige the driver of each government vehicle to transcribe or summarize all the trip tickets issued during the month in the Monthly Report of Official Travels in chronological order to support the reasonableness and propriety of gasoline expenses.

We recommended that Management require the Human Resource Management Assistant (HRMA) to process the requests for monetization of vacation leave credits for a minimum of 10 days and maximum of 30 days in a given year and stop the practice of granting the requests for monetization of sick leave credits except on justifiable reasons pursuant to Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292.

We recommended that Management require the MSWDO to provide adequate control mechanisms such as maintenance of ledger per client which will contain the necessary information and expedite the verification and assessment of the eligibility of the client and monitor the frequency of availment of the assistance.
We further recommended that Management instruct the MSWDO, with the assistance of the MHO, to formulate a policy on the provision of medical assistance to clients who already received medical services from the RHU to avoid duplication and to enable the LGU to extend further the medical assistance to other eligible clients who are also in need.

We recommended that Management refrain from granting financial support in the form of per diems and additional compensation to the Liga ng mga Barangay members which is not consistent with the applicable existing laws and regulations and to reduce the allowance of the BNS in accordance with the maximum amount provided in Section 6 of PD No. 1569 to avoid incurrence of additional maintenance and other operating expenses.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to retrieve all necessary documents of the dormant accounts and to cause the collection of the amounts due through issuance of demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and clarifications on the grant of loan to the borrowers/beneficiaries, collection of unpaid loan balances from the co-makers who guaranteed the re-payment of loan and sending regularly of statement of accounts to borrowers.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer, to reconcile their records with the records of the Provincial Accounting Office on Due to LGUs account to facilitate collection of cash deposits amounting to ₱226,079.32 from the Provincial Treasury.
We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to refile the request for write-off of the dormant accounts once the conditions and requirements provided in Sections 7.4 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 are substantially complied.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to request from all depository banks to furnish the Municipal Accountant of complete copies of Credit Memo and Debit Memo every month; and to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the reconciling items duly supported with documents in the books of accounts.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant process the replacement of stale checks consistent with the provisions of Section 104, Volume I of GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the BAC, HRMA to submit the lacking documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to see to it that the documentary requirements are complete before signing the disbursement vouchers and effecting payment.

We recommended that Management require the Co-Chairman and other members of the MSB to avoid the transfer of appropriations for Capital Outlay and MOOE from one item to another except on the circumstances when changes in the annual budget could be made pursuant to Section 321 of RA No. 7160. We further recommended that Management require the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the expenditures incurred in the SEF and see to it that all expenditures are covered with appropriations and for the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management formulate written policies and procedures which will define clearly the flow of activities in the operation of the waterworks system as well as the roles and responsibilities of the assigned officials and employees. These will serve as a guide to efficient and effective management of the waterworks system and to achieve the goal of providing potable water to households and maximize the use of the resources to generate additional income to the LGU.
We also recommended that Management instruct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to provide the necessary budget for the upgrading of the Potable Water Systems in the Barangays of Allabon, Cayungnan, Patar and Macaboboni to Level III in order to increase the number of households benefited by the projects.

We recommended that Management ensure collection of the delinquent accounts on realty taxes through the Municipal Treasurer by adopting the procedures and remedies provided in Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991” to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the LGU geared towards the welfare of the constituents.

We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Point System to update the GAD database and include the information on gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data in the locality in order to provide inputs and bases in identifying gender issues arising from the unequal situation of women and men in the locality. We further recommended that Management identify the PPAs and GAD activities that are responsive to gender issues in order to attain the main intentions in promoting gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfilment of women’s human rights and the promotion of women’s economic empowerment.

We recommended that Management ensure timely remittance of premiums by requiring the HRMA to prepare and transmit promptly the required forms in updating the records of the personnel to the GSIS to update the personal records of employees both in the LGU and GSIS.

We recommended that Management insure the Waterworks facilities, Tourist Sheds and NCDC and other properties relevant in the delivery of public service to provide safeguard against substantial losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, properties brought by fortuitous events by requiring the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to identify the insurable assets and to submit the inventory report to the GSIS and Audit Team consistent with the provision of  COA Circular No. 2018 - 002 dated May 31, 2018; and to instruct the OIC Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds needed for the payment of premiums.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to the provisions of Sections 59 and 66 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) Nos. 17 and Section III (11) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 by instructing the Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule or a complete and detailed computation of the depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment account and to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the balances of the Depreciation Expense, Accumulated Depreciation and the related PPE accounts in the Financial Statements.
