observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with
the procedures provided by existing laws, rules and regulations on property and
supply management to present fairly the book value of the Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE) in the financial statements by performing the following
corrective measures:
a. Create an Inventory Team who shall conduct the required physical
count of all properties at least once a year and to prepare and submit
the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) to the offices concerned as required in Section 124 of
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) to
facilitate the reconciliation of PPE records every year and ensure the
accuracy and reliability of the PPE account presented in the Financial
Statements;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, as
required in Sections 119 and 120 of MNGAS for proper reporting,
control and monitoring of the PPE account; and
c. Take appropriate action against the concerned accountable official/s
for non-compliance and repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements pursuant to Section 127 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to the
provisions of Sections 59 and 66 of the International Public Sector Accounting
Standards (IPSAS) Nos. 17 and Section III (11) of COA Circular No. 2015-008
dated November 25, 2015 by instructing the Municipal Accountant to prepare
the lapsing schedule or a complete and detailed computation of the depreciation
expense and accumulated depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment
account and to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts in order to
present fairly the balances of the Depreciation Expense, Accumulated
Depreciation and the related PPE accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to
the provisions of Section 20 of Manual on the New Government Accounting
System by requiring the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of
taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Account Register to provide an
accurate basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property and special
education taxes at the beginning of the year and to facilitate the reconciliation of
receivable accounts on real properties for fair presentation of the RPT and SET
receivables in the Financial Statements.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of the
dormant accounts and cause the collection of the amounts due by notifying the
accountable officers concerned through the issuance of demand letters. It was
also recommended that the request for write-off may be refiled by Management
once the conditions and requirements provided in Sections 7.4 and 8.3 of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 are substantially complied.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with
the provisions of Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 by requiring
the Municipal Treasurer to request from all depository banks to furnish the
Municipal Accountant of complete copies of Credit Memo and Debit Memo
every month; and to direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately record all
valid reconciling items duly supported with documents in the books of accounts
to present the fairly the balance of the Cash in Bank account in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to the
provisions of Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 by directing the
Municipal Accountant to analyze the affected transactions and prepare the
corresponding adjustments consistent with the Revised Chart of Accounts for
Local Government Units prescribed by COA Circular 2015-009 pursuant to
Section 113 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 in order to present the accurate
balance of the reciprocal accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all collecting
officers remit their collections to the Treasurer’s Office within the prescribed
period in compliance with Section 29, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual to
safeguard the government funds against possible loss or misuse. It was also
recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor all collecting officers in the
remittance of their daily collections to avoid delay considering that the place of
their assignment is just nearby the Treasury Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refrain from selling the
cash tickets to the collecting officers in compliance with the provisions of Section
74, Volume I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and
Section 29, Volume I of Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS) to provide adequate controls on the issuance and custody of cash
tickets to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict
compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and the
fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of
P.D. No. 1445; and:
a. Instruct the BAC members, BAC Secretariat, TWG, Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4
(6) of P.D. No. 1445; and
b. Impose liquidated damages equivalent to at least equal to one-tenth
of one percent (0.001) of the cost of the unperformed portion for
every day of delay in the absence of the required supporting
documents for time extension in the Construction of Portland
Cement Concrete Pavement (PCCP) at Purok Arinaar in Barangay
Magsaysay.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict
compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (R.A) No. 9184 and the
fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445; and to instruct the BAC and the Municipal
Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD
No. 1445.

We further recommended that the Management impose liquidated
damages against the supplier concerned by undertaking all the remedies to
mitigate the effects of delay in the project implementation pursuant to Annex
“D” Items 3.1 and 3.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict
compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A.
No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in
Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445; and to instruct the BAC, the BAC Secretariat,
TWG, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the
required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
to return to the Source Agency all unexpended balances of fund transfers from
National Government Agencies after completion of the intended projects in
compliance with the provisions of Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001 to enable the National Government to use the funds
for other purposes which are beneficial to the public.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive engage the services of
consultants only when the relevant experience, level of expertise or attention
required for the government project or related activities is not available in the
agency and are not redundant to the functions of the existing offices or regular
employees of the local government unit in compliance with the provisions of
Annex “B”, General Principles of Consulting Services, of the 2016 Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), Section 7 of CSC, COA and DBM
Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid incurrence
of unnecessary expenditures.

It was also recommended that the required documents be submitted by
the officials and employees concerned pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential
Decree (PD) No. 1445 for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a
checklist of supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012 to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents
before certifying in the Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to the
guidelines for the prevention and disallowance of irregular, unnecessary,
excessive, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures (IUEEUE) as provided
in COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and to cause the refund
of the mobile load subsidy totaling to P91,500.0 received by the claimants.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the adherence to
the provision of Section 16 of Republic Act 9003, otherwise known as
“Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” by causing the immediate
completion and approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan which
shall serve as the foundation, guide and timetable in the identification,
implementation and operation of solid waste management programs; and
requiring the concerned officials and employees to strictly observe the criteria
provided in Sections 41 and 42 of RA 9003 in the establishment and operation of
Sanitary Landfill to ensure the protection of public health and its environment.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal School Board
(MSB) to reorganize the composition of the MSB by including the SK Federation
President as one of the members for proper representation of the youth in the
functions of the MSB in compliance with Section 98(3) of Republic Act. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict adherence
to the provisions of Sections 100(c) and 272 of the Local Government Code of
1991 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017 by
utilizing the Special Education Fund within the allowable expenses only and
avoiding disbursement of funds for the programs provided with separate funds
by the Department of Education in order to maximize the purpose of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict
compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of
Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial
transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445; and to
instruct the BAC to be more cautious in preparing the bidding documents and to
require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to submit the
required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure strict
compliance with the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 by requiring the
officials and employees concerned to submit the lacking documents; and for the
Municipal Accountant to see to it that the documentary requirements provided
in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11 Series of 2019 in the grant of financial
assistance are complied with before certifying in the Disbursement Voucher that
documents are complete and proper.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that claims for RATA are supported with complete documentation before
certifying and effecting payment to support the propriety of the claims and avoid
the issuance of notice of suspension and/or notice of disallowance of the
transactions in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert utmost effort to submit
the accounts and financial reports within the prescribed period for timely
examination and verification of accounts and financial report in compliance with
Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of
Accounts, Section 39 (1) and (3) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Article
218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure
compliance with the requirements prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular
No. 2009-001 and cause the submission of copies of contracts to the Audit Team
to enable the auditor to conduct timely review and technical evaluation of the
contracts.

Further, take appropriate administrative disciplinary action as provided
in Section 4 of the same Circular against the responsible officer concerned for
unjustified non-compliance with the Circular.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and
submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity the Procurement and Monitoring
Report and submit the same to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the
2019 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA)
No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 03-2015 dated March 20, 2015.

Further, we recommended that the BAC furnish the Audit Team copy of
the said report to facilitate monitoring on the status of the procurement process
undertaken and serve as a basis in the conduct of contract review on the
procured goods and infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of public infrastructures on the road networks and record in the books of accounts its equivalent amount and disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to Items VI (2) and IX (3) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
 Submit the complete documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of contract and for the payment of claims in compliance with COA Memo Circular No. 2005-027 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
 Ensure that 10% retention money is being deducted from the progress payment to the contractor to serve as warranty for hidden defects during the construction period and shall only be released upon final acceptance of the project and/or if the contractor request for the substitution of the retention money for each progress billing; and 1% retention money deducted from the total contract price for the payment of delivered goods and shall only be released after the lapse of the warranty period.
 Ensure the submission of all the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents in accordance with the relevant provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the auditor to conduct a systematic and effective review process with a view of generating timely and relevant audit results.
 Require the BAC Secretariat to attach a checklist on all perfected contracts as guide to those employees collating the documents before submission to the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, for the Municipal Accountant to also attach checklist on disbursement vouchers and see to it that complete supporting documents were present before payments are made.
observations.managementAction: Management had been very strict in completing the documents for the payments in 2018. Per verification of the audit team, Management had already instructed the BAC Secretariat to submit all the lacking documents. The BAC has partially submitted the lacking documents as of this date. Per verification of the audit team, Management had already started deducting the 10% and/or the retention money from the progress payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the complete documentary requirements for the proper review and evaluation of the negotiated procurement (small value procurement) and for the payment of claims in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and some of the pertinent provisions of IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive instruct the municipal officials concerned to:

a. Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of
December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken
any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-
end. This procedure is necessary in order to know the physical
existence and condition of the PPE and to identify, segregate and
reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the
count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the
necessary adjustments and to pin point
responsibility/accountability for any missing PPE’s;
b. Maintain/update the Subsidiary Ledgers, PPE Ledger Cards,
Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR’s), and put Property
Tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control,
monitoring and identification of the PPE’s; and
c. Control and monitor the use of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for
semi-expendable items to avoid loss as these are usually small
items.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive appoint a
General Services Officer/Supply Officer to take custody of and be accountable
for all properties owned by the local government unit, to perform all other
functions pertaining to supply and management performed by the local
government treasurer, and to enforce policies on records creation, maintenance
and disposal.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory
committee to conduct the physical count of public infrastructures on the road
networks and record in the books of accounts its equivalent amount and
disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to Items VI (2) and IX
(3) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from the practice of
preparing journal entries creating receivable account and liability account
relative to the transfer of unexpended balance at the end of the year from
General Fund to Trust Liability -DRRM in the Trust Fund, respectively, which
is not in consonance with the aforementioned COA Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the municipal
officials concerned to stop charging expenses not related to disaster mitigation,
prevention, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery and be more
prudent in the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the
utilization of the said fund.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the
Municipality has to develop its own engineered sanitary landfill for future
disposal of residual non-recyclable wastes for continuous delivery of efficient
and effective service to its constituents and protection of public health and
environment.

We recommended that Management follow-up the approved Solid
Waste Management Plan to the National Solid Waste Management Commission
to have concrete guidelines that will be adopted for an efficient implementation
of programs and activities for the segregation, collection, transport, storage,
processing, treatment and disposal of solid waste towards ensuring protection of
public health and its environment.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to account and analyze the
dormant accounts, to determine their propriety, validity and exert effort to
collect the same, otherwise, request write-off pursuant to COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to return to the source agency all
unused balances of fund transfers from NGAs after the implementation of the
project for maximum utilization of fund to finance other programs and projects
for the benefit of the general public pursuant to the aforementioned COA
Circulars.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee / Technical Working Group/ BAC Secretariat to justify the
noted lapses, and comply with the law, rules and regulations pursuant to 2016
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring
Entity the Procurement and Monitoring Report and submit the same to the
GPPB pursuant to IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended that the BAC furnish the audit team copy of the
said report to facilitate monitoring on the status of the procurement process
undertaken and basis for the contract review on the procured goods and
infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Engineer to prepare the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC)
for infrastructure projects in accordance with the latest guidelines prescribed
under DPWH Department Order (DO) No. 197, series of 2016 dated October 7,
2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Engineer to adhere strictly to the above-mentioned rules and
regulations in order to ensure quality of the works done by the contractor and
also, there is an assurance that all projects are implemented in accordance
with approved plans and specifications.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular
2013-004 specifically in posting the required information on billboards during
the duration of the project to promote transparency in government projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC)/BAC Secretariat/Municipal Engineer/Municipal
Accountant to submit (1) immediately the required documents; (2)
justification/explanation on the noted lapses; and (3) adhere strictly to the
above-cited laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that claims for RATA are
supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment
to avoid the issuance of notice of suspension and/or notice of disallowance of the
transactions in audit.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the
Local Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat to submit the perfected
contracts together with the documentary requirements enumerated in the
checklists for submission to the COA Audit Team for auditorial and technical
evaluation.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that
management see to it that Gender and Development (GAD) Plan of the agency
will fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives towards the
role of women in becoming a participative partner in nation building and
development, with the end view of protecting their lives and rights and to live a
modest life in a society.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the municipal
officials concerned to implement the planned programs, activities and projects
for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery to strengthen a
safer, adaptive and resilient community towards sustainable development and
also intended benefits would be fully achieved by its constituents.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended the following:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end.  This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets to come up with the RPCPPE and to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and to facilitate in identifying them.
For control and monitoring make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually small items.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the misclassified accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to effect the corrections of misclassified payroll transactions and see to it that all accounting entries made by subordinates were properly reviewed before final entry to the books of accounts as well as in the accounting reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make an extra effort for a detailed review of the dormant accounts and the unsupported balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, when appropriate. Stop the practice of preparing journal entries to window dress the balance of account Trust Liability–DRRM account (2-04-01-020) under Trust Fund book in contrary with the established accounting rules and practices; and
Submit the documentary requirements to support the payment of claims under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for legality and validity of the transactions.

Likewise, for the Municipal Accountant to also attach checklist on disbursement vouchers and see to it that complete supporting documents were present before payments are made.

We recommended that management established their own sanitary landfill as required in the above provisions of law to improve the existing dump site in addressing common solid waste management problems and/or establishing common waste disposal facilities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instill to all LGU officials and employees the necessity of submitting complete and proper documentation for all claims and requiring the liquidation of cash advances immediately before granting another one pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-004 and COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, require the Office of the Accountant to strictly check details of the vouchers and supporting documents, and to submit the same within the prescribed period.
Furthermore, submit the necessary travel orders and certificates of appearance of municipal escorts and drivers to substantiate the relative claims paid to them.

Cause the refund of the unauthorized cost of in-land transportation and meal allowances.

We reiterated our recommendation that management:

Strictly observe the rules in the procurement of services or rental of venues in conformity with RA 9184.

We have recommended that management stop the practice of incurring expenditures more than what was received out of fund transfer and strictly adhere with the trust fund principles as provided under Section 4 of PD 1445.  Hence, disbursements arising from fund transfers/trust fund account should always be utilized only for the purpose it was created.
We also recommended that management submit the documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of contract as mentioned in COA Memorandum Circular No. 2005-027 and for payment of claims under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We have recommended that management stop charging unrelated expenses to the Local Development Fund which is not supportive of the environmental management program of the municipality as required under the pertinent provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2013.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive as the Chairman of the Municipal School Board strictly adhere with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations pertaining to Local School Board Fund and  stop accommodating request from different school for financial assistance that are not within the primary purpose of the fund.  Otherwise, future expenditures of the same will be handled differently.

We have recommended that management submit immediately the required documents to adhere with COA laws, rules and regulations in order to protect the best interest of the government and avoid the issuance of notice of suspension.

We have reiterated our recommendation that the management formulate programs, projects and activities that are gender related to conform with the purpose of establishing the GAD Plan, that is, to address gender issues and women empowerment.

We have recommended that management strictly observe the laws and regulations related to GAD in the maintenance of reports/forms and documents for proper authority, control and monitoring of said fund.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management:
Strictly observe the provisions in the Municipal Ordinance No. 2003-06 or the Contract for Meter Service for the collections of delinquent accounts to avoid accumulation of arrears.
Require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to apply more measures in the collection strategies of delinquent accounts and to include in the back accounts the surcharge of twenty-five (25%) percent on the total water bills due.
Enforce the sanctions provided in Annex B.1 I on the Contract for Meter Service, if warranted, which states that “service connection of the consumer will be cut-off anytime with or without the notice three days past the delinquency date should she/he fails to pay the whole amount of water bill/s and the necessary charges.

We have recommended that management require the suppliers/contractors on the issuance of Sales Invoice and/or Official Receipts on various procurement of goods made in compliance with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 and likewise, strictly observed said regulations on future disbursements made.

We have recommended that management require all concerned officials and employees to apply the appropriate withholding tax rates on all government purchases in accordance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 to prevent revenue loss and to avoid possible penalties arising from non-compliance thereof.

We have recommended that management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer as well as the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain complete reports/records for the proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation of accounting records with the physical count will be completed. Likewise, submit copy of RPCPPE within the deadline as set forth in the pertinent provisions of MNGAS.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a schedule of depreciation for depreciable assets in order for the audit team to verify the validity and accuracy of computation and recording of properties to reflect a more accurate valuation of various PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015 | Management assured the audit team that outmost effort will be done for the maintenance of complete reports/records for the proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation of accounting records with the physical count will tally.




Management informed the audit team that schedule of depreciation was not prepared although monthly depreciation of PPE has been taken up in the books.
observations.reason: The Inventory Team created for the purpose has only submitted its report in CY 2017, however, reconciliation of physical count with accounting record still shows a difference.  Further, observations made on the conduct of the physical count of PPEs disclosed non-implementation of internal control wherein the Inventory Team who conducted the physical count were also the personnel of accounting department who records the additions/ deductions on PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

•	For expenditures charged to GAD fund, indicate in the disbursement vouchers or in the Obligation Request to where the expenses were charged for easy referencing and verification of transactions to facilitate proper matching of budget and actual expenditures.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management as requested the concerned employees who is responsible in the preparation of GAD Accomplishment to comply with all the necessary information as required by COA.
observations.reason: Numerous follow ups were made by the audit team, however, the necessary information needed were not provided by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:

•	Strictly observe the provisions in the Municipal Ordinance No. 2003-06 or the Contract for Meter Service for the collections of delinquent accounts to avoid accumulation of arrears.

•	Enforce the sanctions provided in Annex B.1 (e) on the Contract for Meter Service, if warranted, which states that “service connection of the consumer will be cut-off anytime with or without the notice three days past the delinquency date should she/he fails to pay the whole amount of water bill/s and the necessary charges.”

observations.reason: Although management implemented said Municipal Ordinance and the charging of surcharge, there still the occurrence of delinquent accounts, thus audit finding was reiterated in CY 2016 audit report.
